item.title: Every Frame Perfect
item.description: How imprecise UI animations erode trust in product
item.author: (https://www.facebook.com/nikitonsky)

item.title: Banning noise will be a disaster for statistical data products - Ted is writing things
item.description: Sadly, taking away valuable disclosure avoidance tools doesn't make fundamental trade-offs go away.

item.title: 21 Zero-Days in FFmpeg | depthfirst
item.description: depthfirst's production autonomous security agent discovered 21 zero-day vulnerabilities in FFmpeg, after intensive security analysis by Google and Anthropic. Moving beyond theoretical analysis, our agent produces concrete, reproducible PoC inputs to confirm its findings at a fraction of the costs ($1k vs. $10k). Several of the findings had been sitting latent for 15 to 20 years. We explored the exploitability of the issues and developed a PoC demonstrating a RCE exploit primitive.

item.title: Leaving Mozilla
item.description: > On burn-out and bull-headedness

item.title: Tech Things: There is a massive shadow hanging over this Fable thing
item.description: Man, I was just trying to relax and (have my agent) code on a Friday

item.title: Opensource AI Must Win
item.description: Civilizational intelligence infrastructure must remain free to study, build, deploy, and run, not rented from closed institutions.

item.title: Palantir loses legal challenge against Swiss investigative magazine
item.description: Peter Thiel-chaired data intelligence group sued publication over reports that Switzerland rejected its approaches

item.title: All about electric motors with no rare earths
item.description: As a pioneer of electric motors using no rare earths, Renault Group is unique on the automotive market, a position reasserted for strategic reasons.

item.title: How to Setup a Local Coding Agent on macOS
item.description: Running Gemma 4 26B-A4B and Qwen3.6 35B-A3B locally with llama.cpp, MTP speculative decoding, multimodal support, and PI as a coding agent.

item.title: Statement on the US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5
item.description: The US government has issued an export control directive to suspend all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 by any foreign national, whether inside or outside the United States.

item.title: I Am Not a Reverse Centaur
item.description: About a year ago I wrote on this blog about how coding with LLMs would not work for me, even if there were no ethical or environmental concerns preventing me to use them. I'm not going to repeat the…

item.title: “Don’t You Just Upload It to ChatGPT?”
item.description: Can ChatGPT replace translators? A freelance translator shares a funny Ottawa story about AI translation, professional judgment and why human expertise still matters.

item.title: F-bombs don’t make LLMs smarter
item.description: Imagine someone asks you to solve a math puzzle right after you stepped on a LEGO brick. Are you going to do better or worse than the baseline? Relatedly, can we make LLMs step on LEGO bricks?

item.title: How to Build an Effective Lexicon for Product Teams - scapellato.dev
item.description: How simple event design creates clarity across product, data, and engineering

item.title: Illuminated Computing | My Claude Code Setup
item.description: I seem to have an unusual way of running Claude Code, but I find it a good trade-off between convenience and security.

item.title: let’s do postfix again – brokkr.net
item.description: The mailboxes are from Denmark. That’s the joke.

item.title: Vibe Trading - Gibberish and Stuff
item.description: The old internet was a niche community. Only a few nerds were there. Then every September, a batch of university freshmen would join, causing a lot of annoyance. But it was okay, because they...

item.title: Introduction to UEFI HTTP(S) boot with Qemu/OVMF
item.description: The historic go-to solution for network booting is PXE. PXE is based on DHCP and TFTP. It is tricky to correctly configure, even trickier to make it highly available and good luck with the security with this clear-text unsigned protocol.

item.title: Sandboxes Are Not Security - Jelmer Snoeck
item.description: Software Engineer writing about infrastructure, Kubernetes, and Go

item.title: Twenty Years of Stacking Commits — jd:/dev/blog
item.description: Every code review tool I've used in twenty years has tried to answer the same question. AI just made the answer unavoidable.
item.author: (https://julien.danjou.info/about)

item.title: My Struggles Talking to An Old Piece Of Junk (Fanuc 0M) | Nia Schlegel
item.description: This February I became the proud owner of an old Hermle UWF 851 vertical machining center. An industrial CNC mill with tool changer and everything. I already had a FoxAlien Masuter Pro (which seems laughable in comparison) and a lot of 3D-printing experience so I knew GCode. The workflow I was used to was to draw things up in CAD, do CAM (or slicing for 3d-printing) post-process them somehow and load them onto the respective machines. I knew the Fanuc 0M theoretically had the capability to get programs from a serial port with a DB25 connector on the side of the control cabinet but because I bought the machine without ever seeing it running I had no idea how to actually do that. Or how to use it in general lol.

item.title: Plate Flip
Commits
item.description: A new code world

item.title: Building a tiny FUSE filesystem
item.description: Building a small filesystem in Rust with metadata in JSON and file contents in plain local files, using FUSE to explore inodes, caching, and what it means for a write to become durable.

item.title: How it feels to be an old school web-based sports sim dev when tons of vibecoded web-based sports sims are being released « Blog « ZenGM
item.description: Back when I started working on the web-based version of Basketball GM in 2012, web development was very different. npm was brand new and hardly anyone used it. Bundling/deploying JS was very primitive, a lot of people still were just concatenating files together. And things like TypeScript, React, async/await... these did not even exist yet!

item.title: 4× RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell on Water, and the One Card That Wouldn't Behave
item.description: Converting four RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell cards to waterblocks, finding a VRM choke loose on the workbench, and getting back to 41k tok/s.

item.title: AI juries
item.description: This is part of a series I’m writing on generative AI.

item.title: Ignore what everyone else is doing
item.description: I check Hacker News and Lobsters all the time. It's sort of like an addiction, or maybe just a bad habit. It started about three years ago when I would post blog links on the two platforms. Which Lobsters banned me for. Apparently self promotion is wrong. Well I have no friends or colleagues to post for me, so it's self promotion or no promotion. Anyway, there was a time when I would occasionally find a gem from a dev blog on those sites. This fuelled the dopamine hit and got me coming back for more. Over time I found increasingly less content that was in anyway useful or insightful.
